name: Junbo He
title: AIPM / Computer Science Student / AI-native Product Explorer
tagline: I use AI across user research, product definition, and code delivery, and I am currently building a fitness app designed specifically for gym users.
bio: I am currently a sophomore majoring in Computer Science and Technology. I treat coding, product building, and blogging as one connected growth system: real projects sharpen my judgement, and the lessons become reusable content assets.

What I care about most is how product work changes in the AI era. For me, AIPM is not a label but an evolving practice: using AI for research capture, product definition, document generation, design translation, and code implementation.

profileHighlights.label: Current identity
profileHighlights.value: Sophomore in Computer Science and Technology
profileHighlights.detail: I want my academic training in engineering to turn into real products and lasting capabilities.

profileHighlights.label: Long-term goal
profileHighlights.value: An AIPM explorer for the AI era
profileHighlights.detail: I am tracking how AI changes the way product managers research, decide, collaborate, and ship.

profileHighlights.label: Current product
profileHighlights.detail: Built only for gym users, deliberately narrowed down, and currently under development.

profileHighlights.label: Content direction
profileHighlights.detail: I want projects, books, methods, and growth to become assets I can continuously reuse and improve.

focusAreas.title: Gym-specific products
focusAreas.description: I want to design software around the real flow of gym users and keep only the features that truly matter inside that context.

focusAreas.title: AI-native product workflow
focusAreas.description: I treat AI as a participant in the process so research, documents, design intent, and code stay connected.

focusAreas.title: Reusable content systems
focusAreas.description: I want the blog to be more than a portfolio page: it should become a durable system for projects, reading, and methods.

workflow.title: AI-native product workflow
workflow.description: I prefer treating AI as part of the product team instead of a one-off efficiency tool. The current method is built around real demand, structured documents, and direct delivery.

steps.title: Use AI crawlers to capture demand
steps.description: I start by collecting recurring complaints, needs, and patterns from gym users so product decisions begin with real samples instead of assumptions.

steps.title: Let AI define the product question
steps.description: Based on the demand data, I use AI to pressure-test why the product should exist, who it serves, how it can make money, and which features are truly necessary.

steps.title: Chain MRD through MVP
steps.description: I keep one connected context moving from MRD to BRD to PRD to MVP scope so product meaning does not get lost between documents.

steps.title: Complete the core version in one pass
steps.description: I prefer clarifying the key loop first and then finishing the core version more completely, instead of relying on frequent version updates to repair direction.

steps.title: Turn markdown into AI-readable design
steps.description: Instead of stopping at a traditional prototype, I express design intent in structured markdown so AI can read and act on it directly.

steps.title: Translate HTML prototypes into production code
steps.description: When I need visual validation, I let AI convert prototype intent into HTML first, then keep pushing it into actual frontend code.

principles: Only build what is truly necessary for gym users instead of copying broad fitness apps.
Clarify user value and transaction logic before expanding features or interface detail.
Keep important outputs structured so they are easier to reuse, review, and maintain.
Let the blog evolve alongside the product so the method becomes part of a lasting personal asset.

now: Building a fitness app specifically for gym users and trying to remove as much unnecessary complexity as possible.
Refining a reusable method built around AI crawling, demand data, product definition, document generation, and code delivery.
Turning the personal blog into a long-term brand hub that can carry projects, reading, methods, and growth over time.

timeline.title: Moved from learning code toward understanding products and users
timeline.description: I began to see that shipping features is only the beginning; what matters more is understanding why people use something and why they stay.

timeline.title: Connected computer science training with product awareness
timeline.description: I kept building up engineering basics while using real projects to test my understanding of demand, structure, and delivery.

timeline.title: Started using a blog to store reading, projects, and methods
timeline.description: The website gradually shifted from a showcase page into a system for long-term knowledge capture and clearer storytelling.

timeline.title: Iterating around gym products and an AIPM path
timeline.description: I am pushing an AI-native workflow into real product building while continuously improving how I present my work and thinking.
